title: GPU not visible on macOS M3 when building own image
body:
### Issue Description

After following the guide [here](https://podman-desktop.io/docs/podman/gpu) I do not see the Apple GPU in the output when running `podman run` with my own image, however if I run `podman run` with the `quay.io/slopezpa/fedora-vgpu` image then I do see the Apple GPU in the output

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create machine using LibKrun
2. Build image using a Containerfile that only contains
```
FROM fedora:40
USER 0

# Install the patched mesa-krunkit drivers
RUN dnf -y install dnf-plugins-core && \
    dnf -y copr enable slp/mesa-krunkit && \
    dnf -y install mesa-vulkan-drivers vulkan-loader-devel vulkan-headers vulkan-tools vulkan-loader && \
    dnf clean all
```
3. Run `podman run --rm -it --device /dev/dri --name gpu-info $imageName vulkaninfo | grep "GPU"`


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

```
karl@192 ~ % podman run --rm -it --device /dev/dri --name gpu-info quay.io/karl/gpu-test vulkaninfo | grep "GPU"
		GPU id = 0 (llvmpipe (LLVM 18.1.6, 128 bits))
GPU0:
```

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

```
karl@192 ~ %  podman run --rm -it --device /dev/dri --name gpu-info quay.io/slopezpa/fedora-vgpu vulkaninfo | grep "GPU"
		GPU id = 0 (Virtio-GPU Venus (Apple M3))
		GPU id = 1 (llvmpipe (LLVM 17.0.6, 128 bits))
GPU0:
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

M3 with Sequoia 15.3

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
If another image works with the same command then this is not a podman bug so I am going to close it.
I don't know what is wrong with the install given the documentation is from podman desktop you may have a better chance to ask there https://github.com/podman-desktop/podman-desktop
